data.title: Intel confirms massive $11.1B investment by US government
data.description:
The Trump Administration followed through with its intent to back Intel (NASDAQ:INTC) as the Santa Clara-based semiconductor firm confirmed a staggering $11.1B investment by the U.S. government.

The funding will come from several different avenues. Under the agreement, the U.S. will purchase $8.9B shares of Intel common stock. This will be funded by the remaining $5.7B in grants previously awarded, but not yet paid, by the U.S. CHIPS and Science Act. Another $3.2B will stem from the Secure Enclave program through the U.S. Department of Defense. The final $2.2B are grants Intel has already received from CHIPS.

"President Trump's focus on U.S. chip manufacturing is driving historic investments in a vital industry that is integral to the country's economic and national security," said Intel CEO Lip-Bu Tan. 

Under the deal, the U.S. will buy 433.3M shares at $20.47 each. This equates to a 9.9% stake in Intel, making it the largest investor in the company. It will be a passive ownership, meaning no board representation or other types of governance rights will be grant. The government will also receive a five-year warrant, at $20 per share, for an additional 5% of Intel common shares, exercisable only if Intel ceases to own at least 51% of the foundry business.

"As more companies look to invest in America, this administration remains committed to reinforcing our country's dominance in artificial intelligence while strengthening our national security," said U.S. Secretary of Commerce Howard Lutnick.

For its part, Intel has committed to investing more than $100B to expand its domestic chipmaking capacity.

"Intel plays a vital role as one of the country’s leading chip manufacturers, and we applaud the Trump administration's efforts to usher in a new era of American innovation in partnership with American companies," said Amazon Web Services (AMZN) CEO Matt Garman.

data.title: Spire Global receives NYSE notice regarding late Form 10-Q filing
data.description:
• Spire Global (NYSE:SPIR) on Friday said it has received notice from the New York Stock Exchange that the company is not in compliance with the NYSE’s continued listing standards due to the company's failure to timely file its Quarterly Report on Form 10-Q for the quarter ended June 30, 2025 with the SEC.
  • Under the NYSE’s rules, the company can regain compliance with this NYSE listing standard by filing the Form 10-Q with the SEC before February 19, 2026.
  • Source: Press Release

data.title: Broadway receives Nasdaq notice for late 10-Q filing
data.description:
• Broadway Financial (NASDAQ:BYFC) on Friday said that it has received a notice from Nasdaq stating the company is not in compliance with listing requirements after failing to timely file its quarterly report for the period ended June 30, 2025.
  • The notice has no immediate impact on the company’s listing or trading on the Nasdaq Capital Market, Broadway said in a statement.
  • As a result of this delay, the company requires additional time to fully complete its review of the financial statements for the quarter ended June 30, 2025.
  • Nasdaq has provided the company 60 calendar days from August 21, 2025, to submit a plan to regain compliance with Rule 5250(c)(1). If Nasdaq accepts the company's plan, then Nasdaq may grant the company up to 180 days from the prescribed due date, or until February 16, 2026, for filing the Form 10-Q to regain compliance.
  • The company expects to file the Form 10-Q at the earliest; prior to the 60-day deadline.
  • BYFC +1.23% after hours to $7.4
  • Source: Press Release

data.title: B. Riley Financial announces Nasdaq delinquency notice
data.description:
• B. Riley Financial (NASDAQ:RILY) announced on Thursday that it received an expected delinquency notification letter from Nasdaq, indicating the company was not in compliance with Nasdaq Listing Rule as a result of the delayed filing of the company's Form 10-Q for the period ended June 30, 2025.
  • Nasdaq has granted the company an exception until September 29, 2025, to file its delinquent Form 10-K for the year ended December 31, 2024, its delinquent Form 10-Q for the period ended March 31, 2025, and its delinquent Form 10-Q for the period ended June 30, 2025.
  • As a result, any additional exceptions to allow the company to regain compliance with all delinquent filings will be limited to a maximum of 180 calendar days from the due date of the Initial Delinquent Filing, or September 29, 2025.
  • As a result of this additional delinquency, the Company must submit an update, by September 4, 2025, to its original plan to regain compliance with respect to the filing requirement.
  • RILY +0.18% after hours to $5.52.
  • Source: Press Release

data.title: Triller Group receives Nasdaq non-compliance notice
data.description:
• Triller Group (NASDAQ:ILLR) announced on Friday that it received a Nasdaq notice due to the company’s non-compliance with Nasdaq Listing Rule because of its failure to timely file its quarterly report on Form 10-K for the period ended December 31, 2024, 10-Q for the period ended March 31, 2025, and 10-Q for the period ended June 30, 2025.
  • On August 19, 2025, the company received an extension letter from Nasdaq, which has granted a further extension of time to enable the company to file the Delinquent Filings on or before October 13, 2025.
  • ILLR -1.20% after hours to $0.5394.
  • Source: Press Release

data.title: Modivcare receives Nasdaq delisting notice following Chapter 11 filing
data.description:
• Modivcare (NASDAQ:MODV) on Friday said it was notified by Nasdaq that it had determined to commence proceedings to delist the company’s common stock as a result of the company’s commencement of voluntary proceedings under Chapter 11 of the United States Bankruptcy Code on August 20, 2025.
  • In addition, on August 20, 2025, the company received a notice from Nasdaq indicating that, as a result of the delinquency in the timely filing of the company’s quarterly report on Form 10-Q for the fiscal quarter ended June 30, 2025, it is out of compliance with listing rules, which require listed companies to timely file all required periodic reports with the SEC.
  • MODV -12.79% after hours to $0.9331.
  • Source: Press Release

data.title: Searchlight Resources announces proposed share consolidation
data.description:
• Searchlight Resources (SCLT:CA) announced on Friday that it intends to consolidate its issued and outstanding common shares at a ratio of five pre-consolidated shares to one post consolidation share.
  • The company currently has 146,546,134 common shares issued. Upon completion of the consolidation, the company anticipates there will be 29,309,227 common shares issued and outstanding.
  • The purpose of the consolidation is to facilitate the company's ability to attract future financings, generate greater investor interest and improve trading liquidity.
  • SCLTF closed -9.09% at $0.013.
  • Source: Press Release

data.title: Lifezone Metals files to sell 2.5M ordinary shares by selling shareholders
data.description:
• Lifezone Metals (NYSE:LZM) filed to sell 2.5 million ordinary shares.
  • This prospectus is not an offer to sell these securities.
  • The company will not receive any proceeds from the sale of ordinary shares by the selling shareholder.
  • Filing

data.title: Strathcona will vote against Cenovus offer for MEG Energy, founder Waterous says
data.description:
Strathcona Resources (OTCPK:STHRF) will vote against Cenovus Energy's (NYSE:CVE) C$7.9B acquisition of MEG Energy (OTCPK:MEGEF), founder Adam Waterous told Bloomberg on Friday, saying the latter's board accepted an inferior offer in an attempt to circumvent his own bid.

Strathcona (OTCPK:STHRF), which owns a 9.2% stake in MEG Energy (OTCPK:MEGEF), will continue engaging with shareholders prior to the September 15 deadline to accept its own hostile takeover offer, Waterous said, adding that if its bid fails, Strathcona will vote its stake against the Cenovus (NYSE:CVE) deal.

Cenovus (CVE) agreed to buy MEG (MEG) in a cash-and-stock deal that values the company at C$27.25/share, with ~75% in cash, while Strathcona’s (OTCPK:STHRF) unsolicited offer works out to ~C$28.25/share after a share price surge since it first took the offer directly to shareholders in May; Strathcona's bid also includes a larger stock component.

MEG's (OTCPK:MEGEF) board, which previously rejected Strathcona's (OTCPK:STHRF) offer as inadequate, "clearly adopted an 'Anybody But Strathcona' view," Waterous said.

But Cenovus (CVE) may need to sweeten its bid prior to a planned shareholder vote in October, Desjardins analyst Chris MacCulloch said.
